metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: GoogleThreatIntelligence data connector with template version 3.0.0

connectorUiConfig.title: Google Threat Intelligence Alerts (using Azure Functions)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Google Threat Intelligence (GTI)](https://cloud.google.com/threat-intelligence) data connector ingests GTI Relevance System Alerts into Microsoft Sentinel. The connector polls the GTI Alerts API on a configurable schedule and stores Relevance System Alerts data in the GTIAlerts_CL custom table, enabling security analysts to investigate threats, correlate incidents, and respond to intelligence-driven alerts within the Sentinel workspace.

sampleQueries.query:
GTIAlerts_CL
| extend severity = coalesce(severityAnalysis_severityLevel, detail_dataLeak_severity, detail_insiderThreat_severity, detail_initialAccessBroker_severity)
| summarize count() by severity
| sort by count_ desc

GTIAlerts_CL
| summarize count() by state
| sort by count_ desc

sampleQueries.description: High and critical priority GTI Alerts
sampleQueries.query:
GTIAlerts_CL
| where priorityAnalysis_priorityLevel in ('PRIORITY_LEVEL_HIGH', 'PRIORITY_LEVEL_CRITICAL')
| project TimeGenerated, displayName, state, priorityAnalysis_priorityLevel, severityAnalysis_severityLevel, detail_detailType
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: GTIAlerts_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
GTIAlerts_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
GTIAlerts_CL
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(30d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

Read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

customs.name: Azure Subscription
customs.description: An Azure Subscription with owner role is required to register an application in Microsoft Entra ID and assign the contributor role to the app in the resource group.

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App are required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: Google Threat Intelligence API Key
customs.description: A Google Threat Intelligence API key and project ID are required. See the [GTI documentation](https://cloud.google.com/threat-intelligence/docs) to learn how to obtain an API key.

instructionSteps.description: >**NOTE:** This connector uses an Azure Function App (Timer Trigger, Python 3.12) to poll the Google Threat Intelligence Alerts API on a configurable schedule and ingest GTI Relevance System Alerts into the GTIAlerts_CL custom table in Microsoft Sentinel. This may result in additional data ingestion costs. Check the [Azure Functions pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/) for details.

**STEP 1 - Obtain Google Threat Intelligence API Credentials**

1. Log in to the [Google Threat Intelligence portal](https://cloud.google.com/threat-intelligence).
2. Navigate to your GTI project settings and copy the **Project ID**.
3. Generate an **API Key** for your project and copy it securely.

**STEP 2 - App Registration steps for the Application in Microsoft Entra ID**

This integration requires an App registration in the Azure portal. Follow the steps in this section to create a new application in Microsoft Entra ID:
1. Sign in to the [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com/).
2. Search for and select **Microsoft Entra ID**.
3. Under **Manage**, select **App registrations > New registration**.
4. Enter a display **Name** for your application (e.g. GTIAlertsSentinelConnector).
5. Select **Register** to complete the initial app registration.
6. When registration finishes, the Azure portal displays the app registration's Overview pane. Copy the **Application (client) ID** and **Directory (Tenant) ID** — these are required as configuration parameters.

> **Reference link:** [https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app)

**STEP 3 - Add a client secret for the application in Microsoft Entra ID**

Sometimes called an application password, a client secret is a string value required for the connector. Follow the steps in this section to create a new Client Secret:
1. In the Azure portal, in **App registrations**, select your application.
2. Select **Certificates & secrets > Client secrets > New client secret**.
3. Add a description for your client secret.
4. Select an expiration for the secret or specify a custom lifetime. Limit is 24 months.
5. Select **Add**.
6. *Record the secret's value for use as the AzureClientSecret parameter. This secret value is never displayed again after you leave this page.*

> **Reference link:** [https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app#add-a-client-secret](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app#add-a-client-secret)

**STEP 4 - Get the Object ID of your application in Microsoft Entra ID**

After creating your app registration, follow the steps in this section to get the Object ID:
1. Go to **Microsoft Entra ID**.
2. Select **Enterprise applications** from the left menu.
3. Find your newly created application in the list (search by the name you provided).
4. Click on the application.
5. On the overview page, copy the **Object ID**. This is the **AzureEntraObjectID** needed for the ARM template role assignment.
**STEP 5 - Choose ONE from the following two deployment options to deploy the connector and the associated Azure Function App**

Use this method for automated deployment of the GTI Relevance System Alerts data connector using an ARM Template.

1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below.

	[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdevendra-chavda%2FGoogleThreatIntelligence%2Frefs%2Fheads%2Fmain%2FData%20Connectors%2FGTIRelevanceSystemAlerts%2Fazuredeploy_GTIAlerts.json) [![Deploy to Azure Gov](https://aka.ms/deploytoazuregovbutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdevendra-chavda%2FGoogleThreatIntelligence%2Frefs%2Fheads%2Fmain%2FData%20Connectors%2FGTIRelevanceSystemAlerts%2Fazuredeploy_GTIAlerts.json)
2. Select the preferred **Subscription**, **Resource Group**, and **Location**.
3. Enter the following information:

	 a. **FunctionName** - Enter a unique name for the Azure Function App (maximum 11 characters).

	 b. **Location** - Azure region for the Function App and supporting resources. Defaults to the resource group location.

	 c. **WorkspaceName** - Enter the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace name.

	 d. **AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID** - Enter the fully qualified resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace for Application Insights. Format: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/{workspaceName}

	 e. **AzureTenantID** - Enter the Azure Tenant (Directory) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 f. **AzureClientID** - Enter the Application (Client) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 g. **AzureClientSecret** - Enter the client secret value obtained in STEP 3.

	 h. **AzureEntraObjectID** - Enter the Object ID of your Microsoft Entra app obtained in STEP 4.

	 i. **GTIProjectId** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence project ID obtained in STEP 1.

	 j. **GTIApiKey** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence API key obtained in STEP 1.

	 k. **GTIAlertsTableName** - Enter the name of the custom table for GTI alerts. The _CL suffix is appended automatically. Default is GTIAlerts.

	 l. **StartDate** - (Optional) Enter the start datetime in yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SS[.fff]Z format (e.g. 2026-05-20T15:43:51.16Z). If not provided, data from the last 7 days will be fetched. Must be a past date.

	 m. **GTIFilterExpression** - (Optional) Enter a GTI API filter expression to narrow which alerts are ingested (e.g. state = OPEN). Any audit.update_time condition will be automatically removed — the connector manages that filter internally. Your expression is combined with the checkpoint filter using AND.

	 n. **Schedule** - Enter a valid Quartz Cron expression for the timer trigger schedule. Default is every 10 minutes: 0 */10 * * * *

	 o. **LogLevel** - Set the log level for the Function App. Allowed values: Debug, Info, Warning, Error. Default is Info.
4. Mark the checkbox labeled **I agree to the terms and conditions stated above**.
5. Click **Purchase** to deploy.
instructionSteps.title: Option 1 - Azure Resource Manager (ARM) Template

instructionSteps.description: Use the following step-by-step instructions to deploy the GTI Relevance System Alerts data connector manually with Azure Functions (Deployment via Visual Studio Code).
instructionSteps.title: Option 2 - Manual Deployment of Azure Functions

instructionSteps.description:
**1. Deploy a Function App**

> **NOTE:** You will need to [prepare VS Code](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/functions-create-first-function-python#prerequisites) for Azure Functions development.

1. Download the [Azure Function App](https://aka.ms/sentinel-GoogleThreatIntelligenceAlerts-functionapp) file. Extract the archive to your local development computer.
2. Start VS Code. Choose **File** in the main menu and select **Open Folder**.
3. Select the top level folder from the extracted files.
4. Choose the Azure icon in the Activity bar, then in the **Azure: Functions** area, choose the **Deploy to function app** button. If you are not already signed in, choose the Azure icon in the Activity bar and choose **Sign in to Azure**.
5. Provide the following information at the prompts:

	a. **Select folder:** Choose a folder from your workspace or browse to one that contains your function app.

	b. **Select Subscription:** Choose the subscription to use.

	c. Select **Create new Function App in Azure** (Do not choose the Advanced option).

	d. **Enter a globally unique name for the function app:** Type a name that is valid in a URL path. The name is validated to make sure it is unique in Azure Functions (e.g. GTIAlertsXXXXX).

	e. **Select a runtime:** Choose Python 3.12.

	f. Select a location for new resources. For better performance and lower costs, choose the same [region](https://azure.microsoft.com/regions/) where Microsoft Sentinel is located.

6. Deployment will begin. A notification is displayed after your function app is created and the deployment package is applied.
7. Go to the Azure portal for Function App configuration.

**2. Configure the Function App**

1. In the Function App, select the Function App Name and select **Configuration**.
2. In the **Application settings** tab, select **New application setting**.
3. Add each of the following application settings individually, with their respective string values (case-sensitive):

	 a. **WorkspaceName** - Enter the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace name.

	 b. **AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID** - Enter the fully qualified resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace for Application Insights.

	 c. **AZURE_TENANT_ID** - Enter the Azure Tenant (Directory) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 d. **AZURE_CLIENT_ID** - Enter the Application (Client) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 e. **AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET** - Enter the client secret value obtained in STEP 3.

	 f. **AzureEntraObjectID** - Enter the Object ID of your Microsoft Entra app obtained in STEP 4.

	 g. **GTI_PROJECT_ID** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence project ID obtained in STEP 1.

	 h. **GTI_API_KEY** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence API key obtained in STEP 1.

	 i. **GTIAlertsTableName** - Enter the name of the custom table for GTI alerts. Default is GTIAlerts.

	 j. **StartDate** - (Optional) Enter the start datetime in yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SS[.fff]Z format. If not provided, data from the last 7 days will be fetched.

	 k. **GTI_FILTER_EXPRESSION** - (Optional) Enter a GTI API filter expression to narrow which alerts are ingested. Any audit.update_time condition will be automatically removed.

	 l. **FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME** - Value must be set to python.

	 m. **LogLevel** - Set the log level. Allowed values: Debug, Info, Warning, Error. Default is Info.
4. Once all application settings have been entered, click **Save**.

**STEP 1 - Obtain Google Threat Intelligence API Credentials**

2. Navigate to your GTI project settings and copy the **Project ID**.
3. Generate an **API Key** for your project and copy it securely.

**STEP 2 - App Registration steps for the Application in Microsoft Entra ID**

6. *Record the secret's value for use as the AzureClientSecret parameter. This secret value is never displayed again after you leave this page.*

> **Reference link:** [https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app#add-a-client-secret](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app#add-a-client-secret)

**STEP 5 - Choose ONE from the following two deployment options to deploy the connector and the associated Azure Function App**

Use this method for automated deployment of the GTI Relevance System Alerts data connector using an ARM Template.

1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below.

	[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdevendra-chavda%2FGoogleThreatIntelligence%2Frefs%2Fheads%2Fmain%2FData%20Connectors%2FGTIRelevanceSystemAlerts%2Fazuredeploy_GTIAlerts.json) [![Deploy to Azure Gov](https://aka.ms/deploytoazuregovbutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdevendra-chavda%2FGoogleThreatIntelligence%2Frefs%2Fheads%2Fmain%2FData%20Connectors%2FGTIRelevanceSystemAlerts%2Fazuredeploy_GTIAlerts.json)
2. Select the preferred **Subscription**, **Resource Group**, and **Location**.
3. Enter the following information:

	 a. **FunctionName** - Enter a unique name for the Azure Function App (maximum 11 characters).

	 b. **Location** - Azure region for the Function App and supporting resources. Defaults to the resource group location.

	 c. **WorkspaceName** - Enter the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace name.

	 d. **AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID** - Enter the fully qualified resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace for Application Insights. Format: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/{workspaceName}

	 e. **AzureTenantID** - Enter the Azure Tenant (Directory) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 f. **AzureClientID** - Enter the Application (Client) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 g. **AzureClientSecret** - Enter the client secret value obtained in STEP 3.

	 i. **GTIProjectId** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence project ID obtained in STEP 1.

	 m. **GTIFilterExpression** - (Optional) Enter a GTI API filter expression to narrow which alerts are ingested (e.g. state = OPEN). Any audit.update_time condition will be automatically removed — the connector manages that filter internally. Your expression is combined with the checkpoint filter using AND.

instructionSteps.title: Option 1 - Azure Resource Manager (ARM) Template

instructionSteps.description: Use the following step-by-step instructions to deploy the GTI Relevance System Alerts data connector manually with Azure Functions (Deployment via Visual Studio Code).
instructionSteps.title: Option 2 - Manual Deployment of Azure Functions

instructionSteps.description:
**1. Deploy a Function App**

> **NOTE:** You will need to [prepare VS Code](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/functions-create-first-function-python#prerequisites) for Azure Functions development.

1. Download the [Azure Function App](https://aka.ms/sentinel-GoogleThreatIntelligenceAlerts-functionapp) file. Extract the archive to your local development computer.
2. Start VS Code. Choose **File** in the main menu and select **Open Folder**.
3. Select the top level folder from the extracted files.
4. Choose the Azure icon in the Activity bar, then in the **Azure: Functions** area, choose the **Deploy to function app** button. If you are not already signed in, choose the Azure icon in the Activity bar and choose **Sign in to Azure**.
5. Provide the following information at the prompts:

	a. **Select folder:** Choose a folder from your workspace or browse to one that contains your function app.

	b. **Select Subscription:** Choose the subscription to use.

	c. Select **Create new Function App in Azure** (Do not choose the Advanced option).

	d. **Enter a globally unique name for the function app:** Type a name that is valid in a URL path. The name is validated to make sure it is unique in Azure Functions (e.g. GTIAlertsXXXXX).

	e. **Select a runtime:** Choose Python 3.12.

	f. Select a location for new resources. For better performance and lower costs, choose the same [region](https://azure.microsoft.com/regions/) where Microsoft Sentinel is located.

6. Deployment will begin. A notification is displayed after your function app is created and the deployment package is applied.
7. Go to the Azure portal for Function App configuration.

**2. Configure the Function App**

1. In the Function App, select the Function App Name and select **Configuration**.
2. In the **Application settings** tab, select **New application setting**.
3. Add each of the following application settings individually, with their respective string values (case-sensitive):

	 a. **WorkspaceName** - Enter the Microsoft Sentinel Log Analytics workspace name.

	 b. **AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID** - Enter the fully qualified resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace for Application Insights.

	 c. **AZURE_TENANT_ID** - Enter the Azure Tenant (Directory) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 d. **AZURE_CLIENT_ID** - Enter the Application (Client) ID obtained in STEP 2.

	 e. **AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET** - Enter the client secret value obtained in STEP 3.

	 f. **AzureEntraObjectID** - Enter the Object ID of your Microsoft Entra app obtained in STEP 4.

	 g. **GTI_PROJECT_ID** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence project ID obtained in STEP 1.

	 h. **GTI_API_KEY** - Enter the Google Threat Intelligence API key obtained in STEP 1.

	 i. **GTIAlertsTableName** - Enter the name of the custom table for GTI alerts. Default is GTIAlerts.

	 j. **StartDate** - (Optional) Enter the start datetime in yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SS[.fff]Z format. If not provided, data from the last 7 days will be fetched.

	 k. **GTI_FILTER_EXPRESSION** - (Optional) Enter a GTI API filter expression to narrow which alerts are ingested. Any audit.update_time condition will be automatically removed.

	 l. **FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME** - Value must be set to python.

	 m. **LogLevel** - Set the log level. Allowed values: Debug, Info, Warning, Error. Default is Info.
4. Once all application settings have been entered, click **Save**.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

This Google Threat Intelligence Solution contains Playbooks that can help enrich incident information with threat information and intelligence for IPs, file hashes and URLs from Google Threat Intelligence. Enriched information can help drive focused investigations in Security Operations.

Data Connectors: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
